title: 10 best herbal creams for insomnia

remedy_properties: Sedative properties: Valerian cream helps to calm the mind and body, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep.
Muscle-relaxing properties: Valerian's ability to relax muscle tension and reduce spasms can alleviate physical discomfort that often accompanies insomnia, allowing for a more restful sleep.
Antioxidant properties: Valerian's antioxidant activity helps to neutralize free radicals that can disrupt the body's natural sleep-wake cycle, promoting a deeper and more refreshing sleep.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, creams helps with insomnia because it contains valerenic acids and isovaleric acids, which have a sedative effect on the nervous system. The valerian root has been used for centuries to promote relaxation and calmness, making it easier to fall asleep. The active compounds in the cream bind to GABA receptors in the brain, increasing the production of neurotransmitters that promote sleep. This natural remedy has been shown to improve sleep quality and duration, helping individuals to wake up feeling rested and refreshed.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: This sesquiterpene helps with insomnia by interacting with GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.
Valeranone: This sesquiterpenoid derivative has sedative properties, helping to calm the nervous system and induce sleep.
Isovalerenic acid: This sesquiterpene has a sedative effect, possibly due to its ability to bind to GABA receptors, thereby promoting relaxation and reducing insomnia symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of valerenic acid, a key compound responsible for the sedative and sleep-promoting effects of Valeriana officinalis.
Leaves: Although less prominent than roots and rhizomes, leaves of Valeriana officinalis also contain valerenic acid and other compounds that contribute to its sleep-promoting properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried valeriana officinalis root from a reputable supplier or a well-maintained garden.
Combine the dried root with 100ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 hours or overnight at a temperature between 60-80°c to create a valerian extract.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container to remove the solids.
Mix 2% of the valerian extract with 98% of a moisturizing base such as shea butter and beeswax in a heat-proof bowl.

remedy_properties: Passionflower: Maypop creams often contain passionflower, which has been traditionally used to treat anxiety and insomnia due to its ability to interact with neurotransmitters in the brain, such as GABA, to promote relaxation and reduce stress.

Valerian root: Valerian root is another common ingredient in Maypop creams that can help with insomnia. It has sedative properties that can help regulate sleep patterns, improve sleep quality, and increase the amount of deep sleep.

Lavender: Lavender essential oil or extracts are often added to Maypop creams due to its calming effects on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress, anxiety, and promote a good night's sleep.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata, also known as maypop, creams helps with insomnia because it contains a unique blend of active compounds, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides. These natural ingredients interact with the brain's neurotransmitters to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety. By calming the mind and body, Passiflora incarnata creams can help individuals fall asleep faster and sleep more soundly, improving the overall quality of rest. This natural solution is often preferred for its gentle, non-habit-forming properties.
remedy_constituents: Harmane: It's a neuroactive compound that may help reduce anxiety and stress, leading to improved sleep quality.
Isopimarin: This sesquiterpene lactone has sedative and anxiolytic properties, which can help alleviate insomnia symptoms by promoting relaxation.
Flavonoids (particularly isoorientin and vitexin): These compounds possess sleep-promoting properties by modulating the body's circadian rhythms and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Passiflora incarnata are commonly used because they contain high levels of flavonoids and alkaloids, which are known to have sedative and calming effects.
Fruits: The fruits of Passiflora incarnata are utilized due to their rich content of flavonoids and vitamins, which contribute to the relaxation and sleep-promoting properties of the creams.

Roots: The roots of Passiflora incarnata are often used because they contain a high concentration of passifloric acid and other bioactive compounds, which help to induce relaxation and reduce anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh passiflora incarnata flowers at dawn when dew is still present.
Dry the flowers in a single layer at 100 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Steep 1 tablespoon of dried flowers in 1 cup of coconut oil for 4 hours.
Strain the oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids after 2 hours.
Add 1 teaspoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of shea butter to the oil mixture and heat.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: English lavender contains antioxidants that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which are common underlying factors for insomnia, promoting a restful night's sleep.

Sedative and calming effects: Lavender's sedative compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety, and calm the nervous system, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep.

Pain-relieving properties: The analgesic properties of lavender can help alleviate physical discomforts that often disrupt sleep, such as muscle tension or joint pain, allowing individuals to relax and fall into a deeper sleep.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with insomnia because its calming properties can promote relaxation and reduce stress levels. The creams contain linalool and linalyl acetate, two primary active compounds that have a sedative effect on the nervous system, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep throughout the night. Additionally, the gentle, soothing scent of lavender can calm the mind and body, creating a peaceful environment conducive to a restful night's sleep. This can lead to improved sleep quality and overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that acts as a GABA receptor agonist, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, which helps improve sleep quality.
Linalyl acetate: A terpene that has sedative and anxiolytic properties, contributing to a calming effect that aids in falling asleep and staying asleep.

Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce stress and anxiety levels, making it easier to fall asleep and maintain a restful sleep.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain a high concentration of essential oils, including linalool and linalyl acetate, which have a calming effect and promote relaxation.
Flowers: They are rich in essential oils, including linalool and linalyl acetate, which have a soothing effect and help to reduce anxiety and promote better sleep.
Stems: They also contain essential oils that contribute to the calming and sleep-promoting effects of creams made from Lavandula angustifolia.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers at the peak of their fragrance and potency.
Combine the dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 50g of base oil such as jojoba oil in a double boiler.
Steep the mixture for 2 hours to allow the herbal essence to infuse into the base oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container.
Whip 20g of beeswax and 50g of base oil together to create a smooth and creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm cream's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting relaxation and reducing symptoms of insomnia by calming the nervous system.
Sedative and anxiolytic effects: The sedative properties of lemon balm cream can help regulate sleep patterns and reduce anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep throughout the night.

GABA-boosting properties: Lemon balm contains compounds that interact with GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that regulates sleep and relaxation, helping to increase its effects and promote deeper, more restful sleep.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, creams helps with insomnia because of its calming and soothing properties. The herb contains rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. The cream's topical application allows for quick absorption, helping to calm the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep. Additionally, Melissa officinalis creams may also help regulate sleep patterns and improve overall sleep quality, leading to a restful and rejuvenating night's sleep. This natural remedy offers a gentle and non-invasive solution for individuals struggling with insomnia.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that helps alleviate insomnia by reducing stress and anxiety through its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Linalool: A terpene that promotes relaxation and improves sleep quality by reducing the levels of stress hormones and inducing a calming effect on the nervous system.
Limonene: A terpene that has a sedative effect, helping to reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to insomnia, and promote a restful night's sleep.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their calming and sedative properties, which help to reduce stress and promote relaxation.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and other compounds that have a soothing effect on the nervous system, contributing to a restful sleep.
Roots: Rich in melissic acid and other compounds that have a sedative and anxiolytic effect, helping to calm the mind and body.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g dried melissa leaves and steep them in 250ml of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to make a strong tea.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a bowl to separate the solids from the liquid.
Combine 100g of shea butter with 50g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add the melissa tea to the shea butter mixture and heat it gently until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture is well combined.
Pour the mixture into a sterilized container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30-40 minutes.

remedy_properties: Sedative effects: Henbane's sedative properties help to calm the mind and body, promoting relaxation and reducing stress, making it easier to fall asleep.
Antispasmodic properties: Henbane's antispasmodic effects can help to relax muscles and reduce nervous tension, which are common causes of insomnia and restlessness.
Muscle-relaxing effects: Henbane's muscle-relaxant properties can help to ease muscle cramps, spasms, and twitching that often accompany sleep disorders like insomnia.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hyoscyamus niger, also known as henbane, creams helps with insomnia because of its sedative properties. The cream is made from the dried flowers and seeds of the plant, which contain scopolamine and hyoscyamine. These compounds work by relaxing the muscles, calming the mind, and reducing anxiety. As a result, individuals can fall asleep faster and enjoy a more restful night's sleep. The cream's soothing effects can also help alleviate restlessness and promote a peaceful sleep environment.
remedy_constituents: Scopolamine (Alkaloid): Scopolamine is a tropane alkaloid that helps with insomnia by blocking the action of acetylcholine in the brain, which can lead to relaxation and reduced anxiety.

Hyoscyamine (Alkaloid): Hyoscyamine is a tropane alkaloid that helps with insomnia by reducing the activity of the central nervous system, leading to a decrease in restlessness and improved sleep quality.
Solanine (Glycoalkaloid): Solanine is a glycoalkaloid that helps with insomnia by exerting a sedative effect, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their high concentration of scopolamine and hyoscyamine, which are known to have sedative properties.
Leaves: Used as they contain a high amount of tropane alkaloids, including scopolamine and hyoscyamine, which help to induce sleep.
Seeds: Used as they contain a significant amount of alkaloids, including hyoscyamine and scopolamine, which aid in promoting relaxation and reducing insomnia.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of hyoscyamus niger roots and leaves at dawn for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested hyoscyamus niger in a warm place for 2-3 days to prevent mold.
Grind 20 grams of dried hyoscyamus niger into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powder with 100 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture for 10 minutes and pour it into small containers to set for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Maidenhair tree cream's adaptogenic properties help regulate the body's response to stress, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, which are common contributors to insomnia.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds in maidenhair tree cream can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to insomnia by disrupting normal sleep patterns.

Sedative properties: Maidenhair tree contains sedative compounds that can help calm the nervous system, promoting a restful night's sleep and reducing symptoms of insomnia such as restlessness and difficulty falling asleep.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with insomnia because it contains flavonoids and terpenoids that promote relaxation and improve sleep quality. These compounds have been shown to enhance blood flow to the brain, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can disrupt sleep patterns. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba creams have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to quiet the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep throughout the night.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-based compounds have antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality.

Bilobalide: A sesquiterpene lactone found in Ginkgo biloba, bilobalide has been shown to have a sedative effect and can help regulate sleep-wake cycles by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters such as GABA and serotonin.

Ginkgolides: A group of sesquiterpene lactones, ginkgolides have been found to have a calming effect on the nervous system and can help reduce anxiety and stress, leading to improved sleep quality and duration.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most widely used part due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
Seeds: They are used for their potential to reduce anxiety and promote a restful sleep, possibly due to their high content of bilobalide, a compound with calming effects.
Buds: They are sometimes used in combination with leaves or seeds, possibly due to their potential to enhance the sleep-promoting properties of the other parts or provide a complementary effect.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 250g of ginkgo biloba extract standardized to 24% flavone glycosides and 6% bilobalide.
Combine the ginkgo biloba extract with 30g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter in a mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 160-180°f for 10-15 minutes to melt the ingredients.
Add 10g of sweet almond oil and 5g of vitamin e oil to the melted mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and allow it to cool and solidify completely before use.

remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Properties: Bitter orange (Citrus aurantium) contains adaptogens like synephrine, which helps regulate the body's response to stress and promotes relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep.
Anxiolytic Properties: The bergamot oil present in bitter orange creams has been shown to have anxiolytic effects, reducing anxiety and stress levels that can contribute to insomnia.

GABA-Enhancing Properties: The flavonoids present in bitter orange, such as naringenin, may help increase the production of GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that promotes relaxation and reduces anxiety, leading to improved sleep quality.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Citrus x aurantium, also known as bitter orange, creams helps with insomnia because they contain synephrine, a natural stimulant that promotes alertness and reduces drowsiness. The citrus scent of these creams also has a calming and uplifting effect, helping to regulate the body's circadian rhythms and promote a restful night's sleep. By combining relaxation and stimulation, Citrus x aurantium creams provide a unique approach to managing insomnia, allowing individuals to fall asleep faster and sleep more soundly throughout the night.
remedy_constituents: Synephrine: Acts as a stimulant and increases alertness, helping to combat fatigue and promote a good night's sleep.
Naringenin: Exhibits sedative and anxiolytic properties, potentially reducing stress and anxiety that contribute to insomnia.
Aglycone of Naringenin: Also known as Naringenin aglycone or Naringenin-7-glucoside, it has sedative effects that can help regulate sleep patterns and improve sleep quality.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They contain a compound called furanocoumarin, which has sedative properties that can help individuals fall asleep.
Barks: They contain alkaloids, including aurantiamide, which can have a sedative effect and help reduce anxiety, promoting a good night's sleep.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh organic citrus peel in the early morning to capture the highest amount of essential oils available.
Dry the citrus peel in a food dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 24 hours to remove excess moisture.
Combine 50 grams of dried citrus peel with 100 grams of unsalted butter in a double boiler and heat.
Add 10 grams of beeswax to the mixture and stir constantly for 10 minutes until fully incorporated and melted.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken for 30 minutes before transferring it to a glass jar for storage.

remedy_properties: Scopolamine: Scopolamine, a compound found in Skullcap, has sedative and relaxing effects, which can help to calm the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep.
Bacalin: Bacalin, another active compound in Skullcap, is known for its anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that often contribute to insomnia.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in Skullcap have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe the nervous system and promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Scutellaria lateriflora, also known as skullcap, creams helps with insomnia because of its calming and sedative properties. The herbal extract has been traditionally used to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep. The creams work by penetrating deep into the skin to deliver the active ingredients, which then interact with the body's natural chemistry to produce a sense of calm and tranquility, ultimately leading to improved sleep quality and a restful night's sleep.
remedy_constituents: Baicalin: Baicalin is a flavonoid glycoside that helps with insomnia by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety due to its sedative properties.
Scutellarin: Scutellarin is an iridoid glycoside that helps with insomnia by reducing stress and promoting a good night's sleep through its anxiolytic and hypnotic effects.
Volatile oils (including Scutellarein and other flavonoids): These volatile oils have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping with insomnia by promoting relaxation and reducing restlessness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their calming and sedative properties.
Roots: They are used because of their ability to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety.
Flowers: They are used for their soothing and sedative effects, which help in inducing sleep.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh scutellaria lateriflora flowers in the morning after the dew has dried off.
Infuse 1 cup of dried scutellaria lateriflora flowers in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a clear infusion.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax with 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat.
Combine the scutellaria lateriflora infusion with the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Sedative and anxiolytic properties: The flavonoids present in Lime flowers, such as kaempferol and quercetin, have been shown to exhibit sedative and anxiolytic effects, which can help reduce stress and anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep.

Muscle relaxant properties: The volatile oils in Lime flowers, particularly limonene and beta-pinene, have been found to possess muscle relaxant properties, which can help alleviate physical tension and discomfort that may be contributing to insomnia.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: The antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds present in Lime flowers, such as flavonoids and terpenes, may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to improved sleep quality and duration.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tilia platyphyllos, also known as broad-leaved lime, creams helps with insomnia because of its soothing and calming properties. The flowers and leaves of the Tilia platyphyllos plant contain a high concentration of apigenin and linalool, which have a sedative effect on the body. These compounds help to relax the mind and body, promoting a peaceful state of mind and reducing stress levels. By applying a Tilia platyphyllos cream topically, individuals can experience a calming effect, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep throughout the night.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds have sedative properties, helping to calm the mind and body, leading to improved sleep quality and duration.
Flavonoids (especially quercetin): Quercetin in Tilia platyphyllos has been shown to have a sedative effect by reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep.
Tannins: The astringent properties of tannins in Tilia platyphyllos help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, contributing to improved sleep and reduced insomnia symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their sedative and calming properties to help with insomnia.
Flowers: Used to create a soothing and relaxing effect to aid in falling asleep.
Barks: Used for their calming properties to reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to insomnia.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of tilia platyphyllos flowers and leaves in the early morning when the plant is at its highest water content.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area at 50°c for 2 hours to reduce its moisture content.
Infuse 250g of the dried tilia platyphyllos in 1l of jojoba oil at 60°c for 4 hours to create a plant oil extract.
Mix the tilia platyphyllos extract with 100g of beeswax and 100g of shea butter at 60°c to create a uniform cream base.
Stir the mixture thoroughly and pour it into containers at room temperature for 24 hours to set the cream.

remedy_properties: Triterpenoid saponins: Motherwort cream contains triterpenoid saponins, which have a sedative effect and can help regulate sleep patterns by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in motherwort cream have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe the nervous system and reduce stress and anxiety that contribute to insomnia.
Cardiac glycosides: Motherwort cream contains cardiac glycosides, which have a calming effect on the heart and nervous system, helping to relax the body and promote a restful sleep.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Leonurus cardiaca, also known as motherwort, creams helps with insomnia because of its ability to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety levels. The herb contains compounds such as leonurine and stachydrine, which have been shown to have a sedative effect on the nervous system. By calming the mind and body, Leonurus cardiaca creams can help individuals fall asleep faster and improve the quality of their sleep. This, in turn, can lead to a range of benefits, including improved mood and increased energy levels.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Iridoids, specifically leonurine and rosmarinic acid, have been found to have sedative and hypnotic properties, helping to reduce stress and anxiety that can contribute to insomnia.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids, such as leonurine's triterpene derivatives, have been shown to have a sedative effect on the central nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing restlessness, which can help with falling asleep.

Phenylethanoids: Phenylethanoids, including acteoside and leonurine, have been found to possess anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties, which can help alleviate the anxiety and stress that can lead to insomnia.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain a significant amount of rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, which have sedative and calming properties.
Flowers: They are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which contribute to their sedative and sleep-inducing effects.
Roots: They are a source of alkaloids like leonurine and stachydrine, which have been traditionally used to treat anxiety and insomnia.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of dried leonurus cardiaca flowers in late summer when they are in full bloom.
Combine 2 tablespoons of dried flowers with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 1 hour then remove from heat and let cool.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl and discard the solids.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the mixture and whip until smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats creams helps with insomnia because of their soothing and calming properties that promote relaxation and calmness, making it easier to fall asleep. The anti-inflammatory compounds present in oats help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common underlying causes of insomnia. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing effects can alleviate dry skin and joint pain, allowing individuals to sleep more comfortably and wake up feeling refreshed.
remedy_properties: Soothing anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal oats creams containing ingredients like oat kernel extract or colloidal oatmeal can help reduce inflammation in the skin, which can contribute to sleep disturbances and improve overall relaxation, leading to better insomnia management.

Calming antioxidant effects: Oat-based creams rich in antioxidants can neutralize free radicals that can disrupt sleep patterns, promoting a sense of calmness and reducing stress levels, thereby improving the quality of sleep.

Moisturizing emollient properties: Herbal oats creams can help lock in moisture and soothe dry skin, which is often exacerbated by frequent waking during the night, allowing for more comfortable sleeping conditions and reduced insomnia.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which have been shown to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety symptoms associated with insomnia.
Rhyzomes: Contains a type of polysaccharide called beta-glucan, which has been found to stimulate the production of serotonin, a neurotransmitter involved in regulating sleep-wake cycles.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 50 grams of high-quality beeswax and melt it in a double boiler.
Combine 100 milliliters of coconut oil and 20 milliliters of sweet almond oil in the melted beeswax.
Add 10 grams of dried avena sativa flowers to the mixture and steep for 4 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 2 hours of infusion.
Pour the infused oil into sterilized jars and let it cool before use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with insomnia because of its calming properties that promote relaxation and soothe the mind and body. The herb chamomile has been used for centuries to treat anxiety, stress, and restlessness, making it an effective natural remedy for promoting a good night's sleep. When applied topically as a cream, chamomile can help calm the nervous system, reducing tension and anxiety that may be contributing to insomnia. As a result, individuals can fall asleep faster, sleep more soundly, and wake up feeling refreshed and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic Properties: Chamomile creams have anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties, which can help individuals fall asleep faster and stay asleep longer by calming their minds and bodies.
Muscle Relaxant Properties: Chamomile creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help relax muscles, reducing muscle tension and promoting relaxation, making it easier to fall into a deep sleep.

Soothing Skin Properties: The topical application of chamomile creams can soothe dry, irritated skin, which is common in people who suffer from insomnia, helping to create a calm and comfortable environment conducive to falling asleep.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of chamomile contain a higher concentration of apigenin, an antioxidant that has been shown to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers are rich in flavonoids and terpenes, which have been traditionally used to calm the mind and body, promoting a restful sleep.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers from your garden or purchase them from a reputable supplier.
Dry the harvested flowers in a clean, dry place for at least 24 hours or use a food dehydrator on low heat.
Combine 20 grams of dried chamomile flowers with 100 ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2 to 3 hours, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax has melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 180°f.
Remove from heat, let cool to room temperature, then strain through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into clean glass jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops creams helps with insomnia because they contain flavonoids, which have been shown to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels. The creams also contain humulone, a natural sedative that can help slow down brain activity and prepare the body for sleep. Additionally, hops have been used for centuries as a natural remedy for anxiety and restlessness, making them an effective ingredient in combating insomnia and promoting a good night's sleep.
remedy_properties: Sedative and Calming Effects: Hops contain flavonoids, such as xanthohumol, which have a sedative effect on the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep.
Muscle Relaxation: The beta-carboline compounds present in hops cream have been shown to relax muscle tissue, alleviating tension and discomfort that can disrupt sleep patterns.

Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Hops contain antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds like humulone, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, promoting a restful night's sleep by reducing pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the hops plant are used due to their sedative properties, which help promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, making them ideal for insomnia treatment.
Buds: Hops buds contain flavonoids and terpenes that have been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to induce sleepiness and improve sleep quality.
remedy_recipe: Gather one cup of dried humulus lupulus flowers and two cups of coconut oil.
Combine the dried flowers with one cup of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for two hours stirring occasionally until the mixture cools.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar adding 20 drops of lavender essential oil.
Allow the cream to steep at room temperature for six hours then store it in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milkweed creams helps with insomnia because they contain saponins, a unique compound that promotes relaxation and reduces anxiety. The cream's calming properties help to quiet the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep. Additionally, milkweed has been shown to have a sedative effect, helping to slow down racing thoughts and promote a restful night's sleep. As a result, using herbal milkweed creams can be an effective way to improve sleep quality and wake up feeling refreshed and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Saponins: Milkweed contains saponins, a type of compound known for their calming and relaxing effects on the nervous system, which could help alleviate symptoms of insomnia.
Alkaloids: The plant also contains various alkaloids, including those with sedative properties, which might contribute to its potential benefits in promoting relaxation and sleep quality.
Amino acids: Milkweed is a rich source of certain amino acids, such as tryptophan, serotonin, and melatonin, which are involved in the regulation of sleep-wake cycles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing alkaloids like cardenolides, the roots help regulate sleep patterns and reduce anxiety.
Seeds: Rich in saponins, seeds promote relaxation and soothe the nervous system, making it easier to fall asleep.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh asclepias syriaca flowers and leaves in the early morning hours for optimal potency and aroma.
Dry the harvested botanicals using a food dehydrator on low heat for at least six hours or until crumbly dry texture is achieved.
Grind the dried asclepias syriaca botanicals into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for approximately 15 minutes to release essential oils and compounds.
Mix one tablespoon of the powdered asclepias syriaca with two tablespoons of cold-pressed coconut oil in a double boiler and heat for 30 minutes or until well combined and slightly warm.
Remove from heat and let cool before transferring to glass containers for storage, allowing the cream to mature for at least 24 hours before using on skin for insomnia relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red shiso creams helps with insomnia because they promote relaxation and calmness, allowing individuals to drift off to sleep more easily. The herbal extract has a soothing effect on the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety that can interfere with sleep. Additionally, red shiso creams have natural anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate any physical discomfort that could be causing restlessness or insomnia. By incorporating these creams into one's bedtime routine, individuals can wake up feeling refreshed and rejuvenated, ready to take on the day.
remedy_properties: Sedative and Calming Properties: The essential oils present in red shiso, such as citronellol and geraniol, have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to calm the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep.

Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Red shiso's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are often associated with insomnia and other sleep disorders.

Anxiolytic and Stress-Reducing Properties: The flavonoids and volatile oils present in red shiso have been shown to have anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects, helping to calm the mind and reduce stress, making it easier to relax and fall asleep.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known for their calming and sedative properties, making them an effective ingredient in promoting a restful night's sleep.
Barks: Containing compounds that help to regulate the body's natural sleep-wake cycle, allowing for deeper and more rejuvenating sleep.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried lithospermum erythrorhizon roots in the morning after sunrise weighing 500 grams.
Grind the harvested roots into fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 30 minutes of harvesting.
Combine 250 milliliters of organic coconut oil with 125 milliliters of beeswax and heat until melted at 75°c for 15 minutes.
Add 25 grams of ground lithospermum erythrorhizon powder to the heated oil mixture stirring well for 5 minutes.
Pour the cream mixture into sterilized glass jars and let cool at room temperature for 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with insomnia because they contain soothing compounds that promote relaxation and calmness. The creamy texture also provides a comforting sensation, helping to quiet the mind and body before sleep. Additionally, marshmallows are known to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that often contribute to insomnia. As the body relaxes, it becomes easier to fall asleep and stay asleep throughout the night.
remedy_properties: Soothing Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The mucilage in marshmallow creams can reduce inflammation in the body, which may contribute to improved sleep quality by reducing pain and discomfort that can disrupt sleep.

Antioxidant Properties: Marshmallow creams contain antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals, which can damage cells and contribute to oxidative stress that can interfere with sleep-wake cycles.
Glycosaminoglycan (GAG) Content: The GAGs in marshmallow creams have been shown to have a sedative effect, helping to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and calms the skin, making it an excellent base for insomnia creams.
Rhyzomes: Contain saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce stress and promote relaxation, essential for a good night's sleep.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried marshmallow root and grind it into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine the ground marshmallow root with 200ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil in a double boiler setup for 10 minutes.
Heat the mixture over low heat and stir occasionally until the mixture reaches a temperature of 60°c to 70°c for 5 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to room temperature, then add 20g of beeswax and mix well for 5 minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized containers and store them in the refrigerator at a temperature below 15°c for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Soapwort creams helps with insomnia because of its unique ability to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation. The natural saponins in soapwort have a soothing effect on the skin, reducing stress and anxiety that often contributes to difficulty falling asleep. Additionally, the creamy texture provides gentle moisturizing properties, calming the skin and mind, allowing individuals to drift off to sleep more easily. As a result, incorporating soapwort creams into one's bedtime routine can help improve the quality of sleep and address insomnia issues.
remedy_properties: Saponin content: Soapwort contains high levels of saponins, which have been shown to have sedative and calming effects on the nervous system, helping to promote relaxation and reduce stress that can contribute to insomnia.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory properties of soapwort cream may help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing discomfort and promoting a restful night's sleep.
Antioxidant activity: Soapwort's antioxidant properties may help protect the body from free radicals that can disrupt normal sleep patterns, promoting a deeper and more restful sleep.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to calm the mind and body, promoting a restful sleep.
Rhyzomes: Containing essential oils that soothe and relax the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety leading to better sleep.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried saponaria officinalis leaves and flowers measuring 20 grams for every 100 milliliters of oil base.
Combine the dried herbs with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture at low temperatures around 60 degrees celsius for 4 hours to facilitate solubilization and infusion.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container discarding the solids after 30 minutes of pressing.
Add 10 milliliters of beeswax and blend until fully incorporated then pour into glass jars and refrigerate for at least 2 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese aralia creams helps with insomnia because they contain potent compounds that promote relaxation and reduce anxiety. The cream's soothing properties calm the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep. Additionally, Chinese aralia's adaptogenic properties help regulate the body's internal rhythms, promoting a healthy sleep-wake cycle. As a result, individuals can enjoy deeper, more restful sleep, leading to improved overall well-being and enhanced quality of life.
remedy_properties: Sedative and anti-anxiety effects: Aralia plants contain saponins and flavonoids that may help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, leading to better sleep quality.
Muscle relaxant properties: Aralia plants may have muscle relaxant properties that can help alleviate physical tension and discomfort, which can contribute to insomnia.
Antispasmodic effects: Aralia plants contain compounds that may help reduce spasms and cramps, which can disrupt sleep and contribute to insomnia.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used for their sedative properties, helping to promote relaxation and calmness.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes have a calming effect on the nervous system, making them suitable for insomnia treatment.
remedy_recipe: Extract 1 cup of fresh tetrapanax papyrifer leaves in 2 cups of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of tetrapanax papyrifer extract with 10 grams of shea butter and 10 grams of coconut oil.
Combine 10 drops of lavender essential oil with 10 drops of chamomile essential oil and mix well.
Add 20 drops of vitamin b complex to the mixture and stir for 5-7 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a sterilized glass container and allow it to cool and solidify for 30-40 minutes.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for insomnia are topical products infused with calming herbs that help individuals relax and fall asleep. They work by reducing stress and anxiety, promoting relaxation, and regulating sleep patterns. The best herbal creams for insomnia include Valeriana officinalis, Passiflora incarnata, Lavandula angustifolia, Melissa officinalis, Avena sativa, Scutellaria lateriflora, Zingiber officinale, Ginkgo biloba, Humulus lupulus, and Scutellaria baicalensis. If left untreated, insomnia can lead to fatigue, decreased productivity, mood disturbances, and even cardiovascular problems, significantly impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to insomnia that are treated with herbal creams are anxiety, stress, and restlessness. Herbal creams containing ingredients like passionflower, valerian root, and lavender can help calm the mind and promote relaxation. These creams can be applied topically to the skin, allowing the active ingredients to absorb into the bloodstream and promote a sense of tranquility.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with insomnia is a blend of valerian root, lavender, and chamomile. Valerian root cream promotes relaxation and calms the nervous system, while lavender cream soothes the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety. Chamomile cream further calms the body, reducing inflammation and promoting a restful sleep. Applying these creams together before bedtime can help alleviate insomnia symptoms and promote a good night's sleep.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Planta medica, herbal creams containing valerian, passionflower, and ashwagandha can be beneficial for insomnia due to their sedative properties. The study, which reviewed 23 medicinal plants and their combinations, found that the combination of valerian with hops and passionflower showed the best results in clinical tests for treating insomnia. This suggests that herbal creams containing these plants may help individuals with insomnia by promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis creams helps with insomnia because they contain the root of the valerian plant, which has been traditionally used to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality. The cream's active compound, valerenic acid, works by interacting with the brain's GABA receptors, helping to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety. As a result, users experience improved sleep duration, reduced sleep latency, and a more restful sleep, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep throughout the night. Its natural properties make it a popular alternative to conventional sleep aids.
remedy_constituents: Isovaleric acid: It acts as a GABA receptor agonist, which helps to increase the activity of neurotransmitters that promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, leading to improved sleep quality.
Valerenic acid: It has a sedative effect on the central nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress levels, which can contribute to insomnia, and promoting a calm and restful sleep.

Valeranone: It has a sedative effect on the nervous system and is known to decrease the time it takes to fall asleep, increase sleep duration, and improve sleep quality by reducing anxiety and stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of valerenic acid, a key compound responsible for its sedative and sleep-promoting properties.
Stems: They contain a significant amount of valerianic acid, a related compound that contributes to the plant's calming effects.
Leaves: They contain valerenic acid and other bioactive compounds that help in promoting relaxation and reducing sleep disturbances.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots and clean them thoroughly to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the valeriana officinalis roots in a warm place for 2-3 weeks or until they crumble easily.
Combine 20g of dried valeriana officinalis root powder with 20g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture at 160-180°f for 10-15 minutes to melt the ingredients and combine them evenly.
Strain the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and thicken for 30 minutes to an hour.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata creams helps with insomnia because they contain the herb Passiflora, also known as passionflower, which has been traditionally used to calm the mind and body. The active compounds in Passiflora, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, interact with the brain's neurotransmitters to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. This can lead to improved sleep quality by reducing the time it takes to fall asleep and increasing the duration of sleep, resulting in a restful and rejuvenating night's sleep. Regular use can also lead to long-term sleep benefits.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help alleviate insomnia by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety through their antioxidant properties and interactions with neurotransmitters.
Isovitexin: This flavonoid glycoside helps with insomnia by inducing sedation and reducing stress levels, possibly due to its ability to modulate the activity of GABA receptors in the brain.
Hyperoside: It helps with insomnia by promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety, and acting as a mild sedative, possibly due to its interactions with neurotransmitters such as GABA and serotonin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and alkaloids, which are known for their sedative and relaxing properties.
Fruits: The fruit pulp contains passifloric acid, which has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system.
Roots: The roots of the plant contain a higher concentration of passifloric acid and other bioactive compounds that contribute to their sedative effects.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 4 ounces of dried passiflora incarnata in 8 ounces of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix 1 tablespoon of beeswax with 2 tablespoons of shea butter and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Combine 2 ounces of the infused oil with the beeswax mixture and heat until melted and smooth.
Pour the mixture into a tin and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with insomnia because they promote relaxation and calmness, creating a soothing environment for the mind and body. The calming properties of lavender oil can help regulate sleep patterns and reduce stress, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep. Additionally, the scent of lavender has a direct impact on the brain's limbic system, inducing a state of tranquility and reducing anxiety, ultimately leading to a restful and rejuvenating sleep.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can contribute to insomnia.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene acts as a natural sedative, promoting relaxation and reducing restlessness, making it easier to fall asleep.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-anxiety and sedative properties, helping to regulate sleep patterns and improve the quality of sleep.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Lavandula angustifolia flowers are used because of their calming and sedative properties, which help to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
Leaves: Lavandula angustifolia leaves are used because they contain compounds that have a soothing effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce stress and anxiety that can lead to insomnia.
Seeds: Lavandula angustifolia seeds are used because they contain a high concentration of essential oils that can help to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak bloom for maximum potency.
Combine 1/2 cup of dried flowers with 1 cup of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking daily for 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bowl, discard solids.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/2 cup of shea butter with 2 tablespoons of the infused oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis creams helps with insomnia because they contain the soothing properties of lemon balm, a natural herb known to promote relaxation and calmness. The sedative and anxiolytic effects of Melissa officinalis can help to quiet the mind and reduce stress levels, making it easier to fall asleep. Additionally, the herb's ability to regulate the body's circadian rhythms can also contribute to a restful night's sleep, allowing users to wake up feeling refreshed and rejuvenated.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinatric acid: This phenolic compound helps with insomnia by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety due to its calming effects on the nervous system.
Urosolic acid: This triterpenoid compound has sedative properties, which help to reduce stress and promote a good night's sleep.
Linalool: This terpene has a calming effect on the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep throughout the night.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are commonly used due to their high concentration of essential oils, which have a calming and sedative effect on the body.
Flowers: They are used for their fragrance and their ability to promote relaxation, helping individuals fall asleep more easily.
Stems: They contain a significant amount of flavonoids and other compounds that contribute to the plant's sleep-promoting properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves and flowers at peak potency in the morning after dew has dried completely.
Chop 50g of melissa officinalis leaves and flowers into small pieces for infusion.
Combine the chopped herb with 250ml of water in a saucepan and heat it gently for 10 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix the strained liquid with 50g of beeswax and 100g of coconut oil to create a smooth and even consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa creams helps with insomnia because they contain the extract of oats, which has been used for centuries to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality. The anti-inflammatory and calming properties of Avena sativa work to soothe the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that can contribute to insomnia. By calming the mind and body, Avena sativa creams create a peaceful environment, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep throughout the night. This natural remedy can be a gentle and effective solution for those struggling with insomnia.
remedy_constituents: Avenacoside: This saponin helps with insomnia by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, allowing for better sleep quality.
Furanoflavonoids: These flavonoids possess sedative and anxiolytic properties, helping to calm the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep.
Fatty acids: The presence of fatty acids in Avena sativa, particularly oleic and linoleic acids, contributes to the calming and soothing effects, promoting a restful night's sleep.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their high content of a hormone called avenalin, which is believed to have a sedative effect and promote relaxation.
Roots: They are used because they contain a variety of saponins, which are known for their calming properties and ability to induce sleep.
Flowers: They are used due to their content of apigenin, an antioxidant that can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried avena sativa flowers and leaves by cutting them at dawn when they are at their highest potency level.
Steep 2 tablespoons of the dried avena sativa in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its active ingredients.
Mix 1 teaspoon of honey and 1 teaspoon of jojoba oil with the avena sativa infusion to create a smooth paste.
Combine 1/2 teaspoon of beeswax and 1/4 teaspoon of vitamin e oil with the avena sativa mixture and heat it gently until the beeswax melts.
Pour the avena sativa mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify for about 30 minutes to an hour before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Scutellaria lateriflora creams helps with insomnia because of its soothing and calming properties. The herbal extract, also known as blue skullcap, contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that promote relaxation and reduce stress levels. By calming the nervous system, Scutellaria lateriflora creams can help individuals fall asleep faster and improve the quality of their sleep. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to promote restful sleep, making it a popular choice for those struggling with insomnia. It also helps regulate sleep patterns.
remedy_constituents: Baicalein: Acts as a GABA receptor agonist, which helps increase the activity of neurotransmitters that promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, leading to improved sleep quality.
Baicalin: Exhibits sedative and hypnotic properties, contributing to a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps alleviate insomnia symptoms.
Scutellarein: Possesses antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce stress and anxiety associated with insomnia, promoting a restful sleep environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in bioactive compounds, particularly flavonoids, which have sedative and calming effects.
Roots: They contain iridoid glycosides, which have been traditionally used to promote relaxation and reduce stress, aiding in better sleep.
Flowers: They are known for their calming properties, which can help alleviate anxiety and promote a restful night's sleep.
remedy_recipe: Harvest scutellaria lateriflora flowers and leaves in the morning when they are dry and free of dew within 1 hour.
Steep 1 cup of scutellaria lateriflora flowers and leaves in 2 cups of boiling water for 10-15 minutes to make a tea.
Strain the tea and discard the solids then let it cool to room temperature for 30 minutes.
Mix 1/2 cup of cooled scutellaria lateriflora tea with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil to create a mixture.
Blend the mixture in a double boiler for 5-7 minutes or until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with insomnia because of its natural sedative properties derived from ginger. The cream's active compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been found to promote relaxation and reduce inflammation. This can lead to a decrease in restlessness and anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep. Additionally, ginger's warming and soothing effects can also help to calm the mind and body, creating a conducive sleep environment. As a result, Zingiber officinale creams can be a natural and effective remedy for individuals struggling with insomnia.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help in reducing stress and anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols have anti-inflammatory properties that can help in reducing pain and discomfort, promoting relaxation and better sleep quality.

Volatile oils (specifically, borneol and geraniol): These volatile oils have a sedative effect, helping to calm the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep throughout the night.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Zingiber officinale's rhyzomes are commonly used in creams for insomnia due to their high concentration of gingerols, which have sedative properties.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are often used in creams for insomnia because they contain a compound called gingerol, which helps to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 50g of fresh zingiber officinale root in 500ml of distilled water for 2 hours to create a decoction.
Strain the decoction and add 100g of beeswax to 200ml of the infused liquid to create a base mixture.
Heat the base mixture in a double boiler at 60°c for 30 minutes to melt the beeswax.
Add 100ml of sweet almond oil to the melted base mixture and stir well to combine the ingredients.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 2 hours before transferring it to a container for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with insomnia because they promote relaxation and reduce stress levels. The herb's active compounds, flavonoids and terpenoids, have anti-inflammatory properties that calm the mind and body. By improving blood flow to the brain, Ginkgo biloba creams enhance cognitive function, reducing racing thoughts and anxiety associated with insomnia. Additionally, the cream's soothing effects can help individuals fall asleep faster and enjoy a deeper, more restful sleep. Regular use may also improve overall sleep quality.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help with insomnia by promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety, and improving sleep quality by regulating the body's circadian rhythms.
Bilobalide: It helps with insomnia by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which can contribute to restlessness and difficulty falling asleep.

Ginkgolides: They help with insomnia by inhibiting the production of excitatory neurotransmitters, such as glutamate, which can contribute to restlessness and anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most widely used part due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds contain bilobalide, a compound that may help to reduce anxiety and promote a restful sleep.
Buds: The buds of the Ginkgo biloba plant are said to have a calming effect, which can aid in insomnia relief and promote better sleep patterns.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 20g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in 200ml of carrier oil like coconut oil or jojoba oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Add 10g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter to the infused oil and melt in a double boiler.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and becomes smooth and creamy in texture.
Pour the herbal ginkgo biloba cream into a clean container and store in a cool dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Humulus lupulus creams helps with insomnia because they contain a natural sedative effect from the herb Humulus lupulus, also known as hops. Hops have been used for centuries to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality. The creams work by soothing the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety that can lead to insomnia. By promoting a restful sleep, the creams help regulate the body's sleep-wake cycle, allowing individuals to fall asleep faster and sleep more soundly throughout the night. This natural approach can be a gentle yet effective solution for those struggling with insomnia.
remedy_constituents: Lupulones: These bitter compounds have sedative and sleep-promoting properties, helping to regulate sleep patterns and reduce insomnia.
Humulone: A sesquiterpene with sedative and anxiolytic effects, humulone can help alleviate stress and anxiety that contribute to insomnia.
Xanthohumol: A prenylated chalcone with a sedative effect, xanthohumol may help regulate the body's sleep-wake cycle and promote relaxation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their sedative properties, which help promote relaxation and reduce anxiety.
Leaves: They are used due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe the mind and body.
Stems: They are used due to their calming effects, which can help reduce stress and promote a good night's sleep.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried humulus lupulus flowers that have been dried for 2 weeks.
Combine the dried flowers with 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a 100ml ratio.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 2 hours at 60-70°c to infuse the oil.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth into a bowl and discard the solids after 30 minutes.
Whip 100g of beeswax with 200g of shea butter and the infused oil to create a smooth consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Scutellaria baicalensis creams helps with insomnia because they contain baicalin, a natural flavonoid with sedative properties. Baicalin has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to calm the mind and promote relaxation. By reducing inflammation and stress, it creates a conducive sleep environment. The cream's active ingredients also help regulate the body's circadian rhythms, promoting a deeper and more restful sleep. This makes Scutellaria baicalensis creams a popular natural remedy for individuals struggling with insomnia and other sleep disorders. Regular use can lead to improved sleep quality and duration.
remedy_constituents: Baicalein: Baicalein has sedative properties that help to calm the mind and body, leading to improved sleep quality and duration.
Baicalin: Baicalin has a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to reduce stress and anxiety that can contribute to insomnia.
Wogonin: Wogonin has a sedative effect on the brain, which helps to regulate sleep-wake cycles and improve the quality of sleep.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of baicalein and baicalin, which have been shown to have sedative and sleep-promoting properties.
Leaves: They are used to extract flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help regulate sleep patterns and reduce stress.
Flowers: They are a rich source of flavonoids and terpenoids, which can have a calming effect on the nervous system and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Extract 60 grams of dried scutellaria baicalensis roots using 1 liter of water at 95 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.
Filter the extract using a coffee filter paper to obtain a clear liquid solution.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax with 50 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Combine the filtered extract with the melted wax and butter mixture.
Pour the mixture into a sterile container and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.
